title: Target Profiling
module_title: Technology Stack Fingerprinting and Defence Analysis
learning_objectives: Accurately fingerprint web application technologies and frameworks
Identify and classify defensive mechanisms including WAFs
Map infrastructure components and their relationships
Produce actionable technology intelligence for exploitation planning
scenario: Before active testing, the team lead wants a complete technology profile of the web application target — every technology, every layer, every defence.

scenario_context.setting: Pre-engagement phase of internal web application assessment
scenario_context.challenge: Build comprehensive technology inventory while identifying defensive controls
scenario_context.relevance: Essential first step before exploitation; determines tool selection and approach modifications

subtasks.title: Web Technology Fingerprinting
subtasks.description: Use WhatWeb, Wappalyzer, and manual header analysis.
subtasks.detailed_guidance: Run WhatWeb against target with all plugins enabled. Use Wappalyzer browser extension. Manually inspect HTTP response headers, cookies, error messages. Correlate findings.
subtasks.evidence_required: Complete technology stack profile with tool outputs

subtasks.title: WAF & Defence Detection
subtasks.description: Determine WAF presence and identify defensive measures.
subtasks.detailed_guidance: Use WAFW00F and manual testing. Test with known attack patterns. Document response behaviours indicating WAF. Identify other defensive controls (rate limiting, IP blocking, CSP).
subtasks.evidence_required: WAF detection results + complete defences documented

subtasks.title: Infrastructure Mapping
subtasks.description: Map hosting provider, CDN, DNS infrastructure, related domains.
subtasks.detailed_guidance: Identify hosting provider via WHOIS and IP analysis. Detect CDN usage via traceroute and DNS queries. Map related domains and subdomains. Create infrastructure diagram.

subtasks.title: Hidden Asset Discovery
subtasks.description: Analyse robots.txt, sitemap.xml, source comments, JS files, error pages.
subtasks.detailed_guidance: Download and analyse all discoverable configuration files. Mine JavaScript for API endpoints, environment info, debug statements. Collect error page fingerprints.
subtasks.evidence_required: Hidden asset inventory with risk assessment

subtasks.title: Technology Profile Dossier
subtasks.description: Compile all findings into a Target Technology Profile.
subtasks.detailed_guidance: Create professional dossier summarizing tech stack, versions, defences, and infrastructure. Highlight attack-relevant technology combinations. Include recommendations for testing approach.

reflection_prompt: What hidden attack surface did you discover through JavaScript mining or error pages? How would different technology stacks require different exploitation strategies?

quiz.question: Which tool is most effective for identifying WordPress plugin versions that may have known vulnerabilities?
quiz.explanation: Wappalyzer is specifically designed to detect third-party technologies and their versions, making it excellent for identifying outdated plugins.

quiz.question: What does detection of a WAF imply for your exploitation strategy?
options: Modify payloads and test evasion techniques
quiz.explanation: WAF detection means you need to adjust your approach, use encoding/obfuscation, and potentially test evasion techniques before executing exploits.

mentor_notes: Evaluate accuracy of tech identification and depth of defence analysis. The dossier should guide subsequent exploitation phases. Check for strategic insights about technology combinations and their implications.
